title: Natural products from the human microbiota
research_scope: Identification of biosynthetic genes in bacterial genomes
Prediction of small molecule products from genome sequences
Exploration of ecological and functional roles of microbiota-derived metabolites

human_microbiota_context.discovery_opportunity: Genome mining has led to unexpected discovery of numerous BGCs in human microbiota genomes
human_microbiota_context.symbiosis_focus: Shift from 'who's there' to 'what are they doing'
human_microbiota_context.molecular_mechanism: Hunt for mediators of microbe-host interactions

biosynthetic_potential.methodology: Genome mining combined with BGC identification algorithms
biosynthetic_potential.discovery: Identification of BGCs in human microbiota genomes
biosynthetic_potential.prediction: Prediction of chemical structures from BGC sequences

metabolite_classes_covered: Ribosomally synthesized and post-translationally modified peptides (RiPPs)
